task_completion.description: Launch the Oware game in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application is open and the Oware (Mancala) board is visible on the screen.
The opponent's avatar or indicator shows a computer or Tux (the Linux penguin), indicating a single-player game against the AI.
The agent's interactions (mouse clicks) and active turn indicators correspond to the bottom row of pits on the Oware board.
The score counter or captured seed area for the bottom row player shows a number strictly greater than 24 (e.g., 25 or more).
A victory screen, animation, or end-game message clearly indicating that the bottom row player has won the match.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of GCompris.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse clicks and movements are visible on the GCompris GUI, rather than terminal commands being used to alter game state or configuration files.

integrity.description: The game was played genuinely without skipping to an end state.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a logical progression of the game board, with seeds moving between pits and scores updating incrementally over multiple turns.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual GCompris software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The distinct visual style, menus, and assets of the GCompris educational suite are present, not a web browser or a different Mancala application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GCompris official documentation and KDE sources confirm the activity is named 'Oware'.
